title: Skill-First Package Operating Layer
goal: Implement #427-#429 so capable agents are routed toward task-specific package skills while AGENTS.md, WORKFLOW.md, and compact CLI output remain readable fallbacks.
non_goals: Require every agent runtime to support skills.
Replace compact CLI startup contracts with prose-only skill instructions.

intent.outcome: Startup and fallback surfaces make skills the preferred operating layer for package workflows without breaking non-skill runtimes.
intent.constraints: Dogfood package workflow; keep provider/runtime neutrality; do not add a new first-contact surface.
intent.latitude: Choose the smallest machine-readable routing fields and fallback text that satisfy #428 and #429.
intent.escalation: Stop before changing product direction, removing compatibility adapters, or making skills mandatory.

execution.next_step: Archive this completed plan and leave no active execution residue for #427-#429.
execution.proof: proof selected workspace_cli and planning_surfaces; required commands passed.

invariants: WORKFLOW.md remains a router/fallback, not a full operating manual.
Skills are advisory preferred routes; CLI/document fallback remains valid.
Skill routing stays compact and does not load full skill bodies.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Make the package cheaper and harder to misuse by routing capable agents through skills while preserving weak-agent compatibility.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Future agents can discover package-local skills from compact startup paths before falling back to prose.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: Runtime-specific plugin integration and any generic skill marketplace behavior.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: Compact startup paths can advertise skills without a new first-contact surface; WORKFLOW.md can remain a compatibility router.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: start, preflight, and startup defaults expose advisory skill routing with fallback behavior; WORKFLOW.md and payload mirror describe the fallback role.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: None for #427-#429 after final reconcile and summary.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: Return to the remaining open roadmap lanes if selected later.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Implement all planned work on autopilot. Properly dogfood the package this time.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Promote and implement the just-created skill-first workflow lane through checked-in planning rather than bypassing the package.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Check that the run used package surfaces before edits and did not silently expand into all open roadmap lanes.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: Workspace CLI startup/defaults/skills routing, tests, managed WORKFLOW fallback text and package payload mirrors, this execplan, planning state/evidence/reviews as needed.
execution_bounds.max changed files: Prefer under 12 files before generated/payload mirrors.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: agentic-workspace proof --changed; focused workspace CLI tests; ruff; maintainer surfaces if adapters/payloads change; planning surface checks; summary/reconcile.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Changing the skill registry schema, removing WORKFLOW.md, or altering package install layout.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Provider-specific GitHub integration in core, branch switching, or broad unrelated roadmap lanes.

stop_conditions.stop when: #427-#429 are implemented, validated, closed, and planning residue is archived.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: A useful implementation requires mandatory skill support or runtime-specific behavior.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: Work expands into other roadmap lanes or broad planning engine redesign.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: The narrow validation lane cannot prove startup skill routing and fallback behavior.

context_budget.live working set: summary, report, reconcile, skills recommendation, #427-#429 issue bodies, startup/defaults/skills CLI functions, WORKFLOW.md.
context_budget.recoverable later: historical review artifacts and unrelated roadmap lanes.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run, proof_report, closure_check, and closeout_distillation before archive.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Continue from this plan; implement #428 then #429.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Before editing skill registry schema or after validation failure.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Make skills the preferred package operating layer with document/CLI fallback.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Keep compatibility fallback; no new first-contact surface; stay provider/runtime neutral.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Exact JSON field names, task route categories, and fallback wording.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: The clean solution would require mandatory skill support or product-direction change.

invariants: Do not add another first-contact surface.
Do not turn WORKFLOW.md into full procedure.
contract_decisions_to_freeze: Skill routing belongs in compact startup/defaults outputs as advisory metadata.
open_questions_to_close: Closed: skill routing belongs in compact startup/preflight/defaults as advisory metadata with document/CLI fallback.
validation_commands: uv run ruff check src tests
completion_criteria: Startup/preflight/defaults expose advisory skill routing for at least three common package operation categories.
Skill routing includes fallback behavior for runtimes without skill support.
WORKFLOW.md is clearly a router/fallback rather than full operating layer.
#427-#429 have closeout evidence and tracker state is reconciled.

execution_run.handoff source: uv run agentic-workspace start --format json; summary; report; reconcile; skills recommendation; active execplan
execution_run.what happened: Implemented #427-#429 by adding advisory skill_routing to start, preflight startup_guidance, and startup defaults; tightened WORKFLOW.md and the packaged payload mirror to state that workflow docs are compatibility fallback/router surfaces.
execution_run.scope touched: Workspace CLI startup/defaults/preflight output, tests, workflow fallback text, planning state/evidence, and this execplan.
execution_run.validations run: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q; uv run ruff check src tests; make maintainer-surfaces; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json; uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed ... --format json; uv run agentic-workspace reconcile --format json; uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json.; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py.
execution_run.result for continuation: #427, #428, and #429 are closed. Remaining roadmap lanes are unrelated and remain inactive.
execution_run.next step: Archive this plan and commit the completed slice.

finished_run_review.scope respected: yes; the run stayed inside the promoted #427 lane and did not implement unrelated roadmap candidates.
finished_run_review.proof status: passed selected workspace_cli and planning_surfaces lanes; maintainer surface refresh passed.
finished_run_review.intent served: yes; capable agents can discover package skills from compact startup paths, and weaker runtimes retain document/CLI fallback.
finished_run_review.config compliance: Used start, summary, report, reconcile, skills, proof, maintainer surfaces, and checked-in planning before implementation and closeout.
finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: low; all child issues were implemented and closed, while unrelated open issues remain in inactive roadmap lanes.

proof_report.validation proof: proof selected workspace_cli and planning_surfaces; surface_value_review accepted updates to existing durable surfaces; direct_cli_edit_review was addressed as compact runtime reporting rather than command identity/option semantics.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: 172 workspace CLI tests passed, ruff passed, maintainer surfaces passed, planning doctor healthy, planning-surface check clean, reconcile clean before issue closeout. Generated command package static proof passed.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: Implement all planned work on autopilot and properly dogfood the package.
intent_satisfaction.was original intent fully satisfied?: yes for the promoted #427 skill-first package operating lane; no claim is made for unrelated inactive roadmap lanes.
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: The run promoted the lane into checked-in planning before implementation, used package skill discovery and active planning surfaces, implemented #428 and #429, closed #427-#429 with proof comments, and archived the plan.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Reduce total successful-completion cost across restart, handoff, review, and proof loops.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Prefer compact, queryable skill routing over adding prose surfaces.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Did this make the package easier for capable agents without making weak agents fail?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: The parent lane only required skill-first routing with compatibility fallback; both child slices are implemented and closed.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: Tests assert skill routing in defaults, preflight, and start; WORKFLOW.md and payload mirror carry fallback wording; archived plan records proof.
closure_check.reopen trigger: A capable agent cannot discover package skills from compact startup routing, or a non-skill runtime loses the document/CLI fallback path.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Skill routing is advertised in compact startup outputs and WORKFLOW.md is explicitly a fallback/router.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: Workspace CLI tests, ruff, maintainer surfaces, planning doctor, planning-surface check, proof selection, reconcile, and summary.; generated command package static proof.
execution_summary.follow-on routed to: Remaining open roadmap lanes only; none required for #427.
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: Archived execplan plus GitHub issue closeout comments.
execution_summary.knowledge promoted (Memory/Docs/Config): No Memory promotion needed; durable behavior is encoded in CLI output/tests and workflow payload text.
execution_summary.resume from: No active plan for #427; use summary for next selected roadmap lane.

discard.summary: Command-by-command execution detail is retained only in this archived proof record.

continuation.summary: Unrelated open roadmap lanes remain inactive and must be promoted separately before implementation.

docs.summary: WORKFLOW.md fallback role is now explicit in installed and packaged payload copies.

issue_follow_up.summary: #428 closed after compact startup skill routing implementation.

drift_log: 2026-04-27: Promoted #427-#429 after summary/report/reconcile/skills showed no active plan and recommended planning-backed autopilot.
2026-04-27: Implemented #428 and #429, closed #427-#429 with proof comments, and archived this plan.
